name: Oscars & Awards
description: Academy Awards, BAFTAs, and major film prizes

questions.q: The Oscars are the nickname of which major film awards?

source.notes: Presented annually by the Academy of Motion Picture Arts and Sciences.

questions.q: Who was the first African-American man to win the Best Actor Oscar, for 'Lilies of the Field' in 1963?

source.notes: Poitier also received an honorary Oscar in 2001.

questions.q: Heath Ledger won a posthumous Best Supporting Actor Oscar in 2009 for playing The Joker in which film?

source.notes: Ledger died January 2008; film released July 2008; Oscar in February 2009.

questions.q: Who holds the record for the most Academy Awards ever won by any individual, with 22 Oscars?

source.notes: Won 22 competitive Oscars (plus 4 honorary) from a record 59 nominations.

questions.q: At the 2014 Oscars, John Travolta famously mispronounced which actress's name as 'Adele Dazeem' before she performed 'Let It Go'?

source.notes: At the 86th Academy Awards; both actresses later appeared together at the 2015 ceremony.

questions.q: Which actress holds the all-time record for most Oscar acting nominations?

source.notes: 21 Oscar nominations (as of 2024); has won 3.

questions.q: How many competitive Best Director Oscars did Alfred Hitchcock win during his career?

source.notes: Nominated five times; received the Irving G. Thalberg Memorial Award in 1968.

questions.q: What do the letters 'BAFTA' stand for?
choices: British Academy of Film Technical Arts
British Awards for Film and TV Acting
British Academy of Film and Television Arts

source.original_a: British Academy of Film and Television Arts
source.notes: Founded 1947 as the British Film Academy.

questions.q: Tatum O'Neal became the youngest-ever Oscar winner (Best Supporting Actress) at what age, for 'Paper Moon' (1973)?

source.notes: Acted opposite her real-life father Ryan O'Neal.

questions.q: Hilary Swank's first Best Actress Oscar was for which 1999 film?

source.notes: Won her second Best Actress Oscar for 'Million Dollar Baby' (2004).

questions.q: Denzel Washington won his first Best Actor Oscar in 2002 for which film?

source.notes: The same night Halle Berry became the first Black woman to win Best Actress.

questions.q: Which country has won the most Oscars for Best International Feature Film (formerly Best Foreign Language Film)?

source.notes: Italy has won 14, including 'La Strada', '8½', and 'Life is Beautiful'.

questions.q: At the 2017 Oscars, which film was mistakenly announced as Best Picture winner before 'Moonlight' was revealed as the actual winner?

source.notes: Warren Beatty and Faye Dunaway were handed the wrong envelope.

questions.q: Three films share the record of winning 11 Oscars: 'Ben-Hur', 'Titanic', and what third film?
choices: Lord of the Rings: The Return of the King
The Lord of the Rings: The Fellowship of the Ring

source.notes: 'The Return of the King' went 11-for-11 at the 2004 Academy Awards.

questions.q: Which is the only film franchise to have won Best Picture Oscar twice?

source.notes: 'The Godfather' (1972) and 'The Godfather Part II' (1974).

questions.q: What was the first color film to win the Academy Award for Best Picture?

source.notes: 1939. Won eight Academy Awards total.

questions.q: The 2008 film 'Slumdog Millionaire' won how many Oscars at the 81st Academy Awards?

source.notes: Including Best Picture and Best Director (Danny Boyle).

questions.q: What is the stated purpose of the Golden Raspberry Awards (the 'Razzies'), held the day before the Oscars?
choices: Mock the worst films of the year

source.notes: Founded 1981 by John J.B. Wilson as a satirical counter to the Oscars.

questions.q: Who holds the record for most Best Director Oscars, with four wins between 1935 and 1952?

source.notes: Won for 'The Informer', 'The Grapes of Wrath', 'How Green Was My Valley', and 'The Quiet Man'.

questions.q: Gary Oldman won his first Best Actor Oscar in 2018 for portraying which historical figure?

source.notes: Played Churchill in 'Darkest Hour' (2017).

questions.q: Walt Disney's 22 competitive Oscars came from how many total nominations, also a record?

source.notes: Nominated 59 times for competitive Oscars and four more for honorary awards.

questions.q: The Academy changed its 'winner' announcement from 'And the winner is…' to 'And the Oscar goes to…' in which year?

source.notes: Changed by 1989 producer Allan Carr to feel less competitive.
